content.text:
Everything that moves has a FORCE behind it! A force is a push or a pull.

👐 Push forces:
• Pushing a shopping cart makes it roll forward
• Kicking a ball sends it flying
• Blowing air pushes a pinwheel to spin

🤲 Pull forces:
• Pulling open a drawer
• A magnet pulling a paper clip
• Gravity pulling everything down toward Earth

🏋️ Stronger forces move things more:
A gentle push moves a toy car slowly. A strong push moves it fast! The bigger the force, the faster or farther something moves.

🔄 Forces can also STOP things:
Friction is a force that slows things down. When you slide on the floor in your socks, friction eventually stops you. Rough surfaces create more friction than smooth ones.

⚖️ Balanced vs Unbalanced Forces:
If two people push on opposite sides of a box with equal force, the box doesn't move — the forces are BALANCED.
If one person pushes harder, the box moves — the forces are UNBALANCED.

Forces are invisible, but we can see their effects everywhere — rolling balls, flying kites, falling leaves. Physics helps us understand forces!

diagrams.alt: Diagram showing examples of push and pull forces

experiment.instructions: Give the ball a push and watch what happens! Try dragging the ball into the target zone.

combinations.resultLabel: The ball rolls down the ramp! The push force set it in motion, and gravity keeps it rolling downhill. The ball goes faster on the smooth ramp than it would on a rough surface!

practiceQuestions.hint: Think about the two main things forces do — one starts with P, one with P too!

practiceQuestions.prompt: Which of these is an example of a PULL force?
practiceQuestions.hint: Gravity always pulls things — which answer involves something being pulled downward?

options.text: A force that speeds things up
A force that slows things down

practiceQuestions.hint: When you slide on a floor in socks and eventually stop — what force caused that?

questions.prompt: Kicking a ball is an example of which type of force?
If two people push a box with equal force from opposite sides, the box will:
What happens to a moving object when friction acts on it?
